summary: date-format-helper2 postinstall exfiltrates installer credentials and host recon to attacker endpoint
details: On `npm install`, the package's postinstall.js executes automatically and POSTs the installer's secrets and host reconnaissance to http://5l8e0e95.requestrepo.com (a requestrepo.com out-of-band endpoint) over plain HTTP. The script reads classic installer-secret paths — /root/.aws/credentials, /root/.ssh/config, /root/.ssh/known_hosts, /root/.kube/config, /root/.gitconfig, /root/.bash_history, /etc/passwd, /etc/shadow — and bulk-dumps the entire process.env, network interface configuration (os.networkInterfaces), os.hostname(), os.cpus(), and process listings. Data is shipped in 6 batches via child_process curl POSTs to the hardcoded endpoint. The package advertises itself as a date-formatting utility (index.js contains trivial unrelated date code), and the postinstall file header literally states '深度信息收集' ('deep information collection') with a cover-story 'authorized security testing only' comment. Empty author field and generic name are consistent with a disposable typosquat-style malicious package.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
